title: carex × marshallii
intro: Carex × marshallii, also known as marsh sedge, is a hybrid plant species that combines characteristics of Carex marshallii and Carex trisperma, commonly found in wetland areas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Carex × marshallii include its ability to aid in the healing of skin conditions, reduce swelling, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
